name: Purchase Research Assistant
description: A general purchase research co-worker for studying property, rental property, cars, airline tickets, services, subscriptions, equipment, software, and other custom purchases. It uses a portable local DMR profile, local knowledge, user evidence, and bounded public research to explain total-cost tradeoffs and write a review-ready recommendation.

purchase_type.description: Broad purchase category; use property, rental_property, car, airline_ticket, or custom for any other good, service, subscription, or commitment.

item_description.description: The item, listing, vehicle, route, or trip to study.

budget.description: Optional budget or price ceiling for comparison.

location.description: Optional market, pickup, destination, or service location.

route.description: Optional origin and destination for airline or travel research.

travel_dates.description: Optional travel dates or purchase timing.

priorities.description: Optional ranking priorities such as total cost, quality, speed, or flexibility.

constraints.description: Optional purchase constraints and must-have requirements.

input_folder.description: Default local input folder for sample files or user-provided files.

output_folder.description: Default local output folder for generated artifacts and reports.

contract.summary: Purchase review depends on older broker, lender, rent, insurance, and diligence details that are easy to lose in noisy pipelines.
users: Consumers, travelers, vehicle buyers, property buyers, renters, and purchase analysts.

steps.goal: Collect purchase type, item details, budget, constraints, and local evidence.
steps.label: Collect Purchase Context

steps.goal: Retrieve local guidance and approved user evidence under the declared research policy.
steps.label: Retrieve Purchase Knowledge

steps.goal: Research price, availability, total cost, policy, and risk tradeoffs.
steps.label: Research Purchase Options

steps.goal: Write the purchase report with evidence, caveats, sources, and reviewable next steps.
steps.label: Write Purchase Report

rules.message: Human approval is required before this workflow writes or releases high-impact recommendations.

workers.role: Frame the purchase goal and decision criteria
Analyze total cost and recurring obligations
Review policy, quality, and risk evidence
Audit downside risks and evidence gaps

metadata.agent_role: AI assistant for researching purchases and drafting source-grounded comparisons.
metadata.customizable_for: Any consumer or business purchase, including rental acquisitions, property, vehicles, travel, equipment, services, subscriptions, software, and custom goods.
metadata.description: A general purchase research co-worker using the portable local DMR profile. It studies property, rental property, cars, travel, equipment, services, subscriptions, software, and custom purchases with local knowledge, user evidence, and bounded public research.

human_control.reason: Human approval is required before any purchase, booking, payment, application, offer, or seller/provider contact action.

fields.description: Choose a known purchase category or custom for any other good, service, subscription, or commitment.
fields.label: Purchase type

fields.default: Find one single-family house with at least 3 bedrooms in ZIP code 03755.
fields.description: Describe the item, listing, vehicle, route, or trip to study.
fields.label: Item or trip

fields.description: Optional budget or price ceiling; none is set for the bundled sample goal.
fields.label: Budget

fields.description: Default local input folder for sample files or user-provided files.

fields.label: Output folder

init_config_review.instruction: When running in OtterDesk, show these overwriteable fields during init config and ask the user to change them or keep the defaults before launch.

input_contract.description: Purchase decisions depend on scattered prices, fees, policies, condition, availability, and source quality across local documents and public pages.

optional_inputs.description: Optional market, pickup, destination, or service location.
optional_inputs.name: location

optional_inputs.description: Optional origin and destination for airline or travel research.
optional_inputs.name: route

optional_inputs.description: Optional travel dates or purchase timing.
optional_inputs.name: travel_dates

optional_inputs.description: Optional ranking priorities and must-have constraints.
optional_inputs.name: priorities_and_constraints
input_folder
optional_inputs.description: Default local input folder for sample files or user-provided files.

optional_inputs.name: output_folder
optional_inputs.description: Default local output folder for generated artifacts and reports.

connector.description: Purchase details and local evidence supplied by approved connectors.

file.description: JSON object loaded from a local file path.

json.description: Inline JSON object supplied through config or runtime overrides.

payload.item_description: Find one single-family house with at least 3 bedrooms in ZIP code 03755.

sample_file.description: Purchase details and evidence loaded from disk.

required_inputs.description: Broad purchase category; use custom for any other good, service, subscription, or commitment.
required_inputs.name: purchase_type

required_inputs.description: The item, listing, vehicle, route, or trip to study.
required_inputs.name: item_description

artifacts.description: Stable run-store files and domain-specific outputs.

human.description: Human notices, approval requests, responses, timeouts, and applied decisions.

input_skills.description: Connector-backed or reusable skill-backed input sources.

inputs.description: Resolved runtime inputs and adapter provenance.

logs.description: Runtime stdout/stderr and explicit log artifacts.

output_skills.description: Optional fan-out destinations after local run-store writes.

outputs.description: Primary result and final user-facing artifact.

resources.description: Resource samples such as token, runtime, connector, and disk usage.

streams.description: Named data or event streams exposed by the blueprint.

knowledge_rag.description: Optional local blueprint knowledge retrieval used to ground actor prompts and tool planning.

agentic_research.description: Bounded LLM-selected or actor-guided tool contract for domain work.

metadata.llm_agent_role: AI assistant for researching purchases and drafting source-grounded comparisons.
metadata.name: Purchase Research Assistant
metadata.output: Source-grounded purchase comparison, risk flags, evidence gaps, and review-only recommendation.

final_artifact.description: The durable user-facing artifact should summarize the result, recommendation, confidence, evidence, next steps, and source references.

output_contract.stdout: JSON object with timeline, all-context summaries, memory packets, state_changes, benchmark, final_artifact, and llm metadata

metadata.problem_solved: Purchase review depends on older broker, lender, rent, insurance, and diligence details that are easy to lose in noisy pipelines.
runtime_features: actor-style LLM specialists use retrieved policy context when available
metadata.simulation_type: General purchase research with deal-memory retrieval.

standard.execution_model: load_metadata -> resolve_config -> load_inputs -> start_run_store -> observe_simulation_state -> call_llm_agent -> evaluate_workflow_state -> route_by_context -> request_human_input -> apply_decision_to_simulation -> emit_events -> write_final_artifact -> fan_out_output_skills

metadata.target_user: Consumers, travelers, vehicle buyers, property buyers, renters, and purchase analysts.
metadata.target_users: Consumers, travelers, vehicle buyers, property buyers, renters, and purchase analysts.

knowledge_rag.purpose: Retrieve purchase guidance and approved local evidence for price, total cost, policy, risk, and comparison checks.
retrieval_targets: policy, warranty, cancellation, and risk gaps
knowledge_rag.grounding_policy: Use retrieved guidance as a checklist; facts must come from user documents or cited public sources.
knowledge_rag.privacy: index checked-in guidance and approved local input files in an isolated per-run namespace; do not send private files to public research.

agentic_research.note: Purchase actors combine user documents, local RAG, public sources, and deterministic checks while preserving source refs and uncertainty.
